published_prior_art.cite: YuShiTchetgen2025, equation (2.3), defines the simultaneous fortified space but does not give this general weighted-array decomposition or dimension formula.
YuShiTchetgen2025, Assumption 5 and Theorem 2.1, posit and use fortified-completeness injectivity but do not give a finite-support negative-supply-slack impossibility region.
No located paper states this arithmetic monotonicity; it is a direct corollary of the proposed dimension formula and rank barrier.

YuShiTchetgen2025, Remark 2.3, states a 12-by-15 pairing based on B1 union {1,Z2}; the proposal identifies the omitted third ternary complement direction and gives the corrected 12-by-16 ambient count.

AllmanMatiasRhodes2009 and Gu2025BlessingDependence give generic-identifiability methods for other constrained latent families, not a compatible full-cell minor-attainment result for the fortified pairing.

novelty_flags.label: Comparator claim for YuShiTchetgen2025 in comparator_promise_table, related_work, and project_justification
novelty_flags.one_line: The comparison says Theorem 2.1 obtains ATE identification from Assumption 3, Assumption 5, and consistency, but the cited theorem also requires fortified outcome-bridge existence in Assumption 4; state all load-bearing premises.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 (thm:universal-rank-barrier)
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The result is conditional on the selected-component proxy restrictions and full-cell support, and rules out only injectivity of the fortified outcome-completeness pairing.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Under those conditions, negative slack rules out YuShiTchetgen2025 Assumption 5 for that outcome-bridge route; it does not imply ATE nonidentification or exclude alternative bridge routes.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 9-12, equation (2.3), Assumptions 3-5, Remark 2.3, and Theorem 2.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper defines H_gamma by the simultaneous conditional-mean restrictions, defines fortified completeness in Assumption 5, prints a 12-by-15 illustration using B1 union {1,Z2}, and Theorem 2.1 requires Assumptions 3, 4, and 5, plus Assumption 1 for ATE identification.

source_verification_receipts.version: arXiv:1609.08816v4, 28 June 2018; author version corresponding to Biometrika 105(4), 987-993
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper establishes nonparametric proxy identification with at least two independent proxies under a rank condition; no fortified unknown-valid-subset supply count is claimed.

source_verification_receipts.version: Author manuscript for Statistics and Probability Letters 198 (2023), 109836; primary journal text was inaccessible
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article studies identification and inference for counterfactual means when bridge solutions need not be unique, rather than treating bridge nonuniqueness as automatic causal nonidentification.

source_verification_receipts.version: arXiv:0809.5032v2, journal-formatted version corresponding to Annals of Statistics 37(6A), 3099-3132
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper develops algebraic and Kruskal-rank methods for generic identifiability in latent-structure models; it does not analyze the fortified-proxy completion family.

source_verification_receipts.version: arXiv version corresponding to Bernoulli 31(2), 948-972; journal full text was inaccessible
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper proves generic/strict-identifiability results for discrete BLESS latent-variable models and shows that latent dependence can control identifiability; it is not a fortified-proxy rank-attainment theorem.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(547), 1778-1793
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article reduces discrete causal bounding to polynomial programs and supplies automated sharp bounds; it does not derive fortified-completeness supply dimensions or a universal rank barrier.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct every YuShiTchetgen2025 comparison to say that Theorem 2.1 requires Assumptions 3, 4, and 5, with consistency additionally needed for the ATE formula; retain the Assumption-5-specific barrier claim.

Promote the 12-by-16 calculation from a diagnostic proposition into an explicit correction corollary that displays a basis for all three L2(Z2) complement directions and verifies its direct-sum relation with H1.

Resolve the compatible-minor handle on a nontrivial infinite regime, either by an explicit positive compatible completion or by a structural vanishing obstruction; this would upgrade the necessary arithmetic frontier to an exact generic feasibility result.

recommended_next_step: Revise the YuShiTchetgen2025 premise comparison, then advance the retained field-tier finite-support rank-barrier kernel to Stage 0.
